heading: Statewide strategic prevention plan
label: 10643 Statewide strategic prevention plan
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3797y–2 of Title 42

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A statewide strategic prevention plan shall be used by the rural State to assist local communities, both directly and through existing State programs and services, in building comprehensive, strategic, and innovative approaches to reducing crime, violence, and substance abuse based on local conditions and needs.

children.heading: Goals
children.label: b Goals
children.text: The plan must contain statewide long-term goals and measurable annual objectives for reducing crime, violence, and substance abuse.

children.heading: Accountability
children.label: c Accountability
children.text: The rural State shall be required to develop and report in its plan relevant performance targets and measures for the goals and objectives to track changes in crime, violence, and substance abuse.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: d Consultation
children.text: The rural State shall form a State crime free communities commission that includes representatives of State and local government, and community leaders who will provide advice and recommendations on relevant community goals and objectives, and performance targets and measures.
